key_insight: MOSAIC is a two-phase multi-LLM system that can synthesize EHR evidence into multidimensional severity phenotypes, showing that agentic LLMs can go beyond fixed rule-based scoring.
clinical_relevance: For type 2 diabetes, this approach could help stratify disease severity more richly than existing algorithms and may support risk assessment from routinely collected records.
method: Agentic two-phase LLM framework for severity phenotyping
Tested on synthetic EHR cohorts for type 2 diabetes
Compared with multiple algorithmic severity scores and outcome associations
evidence_signal: On SyntheticMass, open-weight MOSAIC closely matched the proprietary version (weighted kappa 0.773) and showed moderate-to-fair agreement with established scores while separating mortality and complication outcomes.
watch_item: Results were based on synthetic data and a single proof-of-concept disease, so real-world validation and robustness testing are still needed before implementation.
paper_title: Multi-Large Language Model Orchestrated Severity Assessment of Clinical Records (MOSAIC)
